params.description: Sticker sheet appears showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: In the Warmup, you saw equal groups lined up into a rectangle. Check out this sticker sheet. The stickers aren't bunched up or scattered around. They're lined up. Every sticker has its own spot. Here's what I notice.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: See how they're lined up? They go across, and they go up and down. Everything lines up into a rectangle.

params.description: System briefly outlines the full rectangle shape around the sticker sheet.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers. Rectangle outline visible around the array.

beats.text: When objects are arranged like this, lined up in a rectangle with every spot filled, that's called an array.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in rectangular arrangement.

params.description: 3×4 array of stickers appears. 3 rows, 4 columns. Neutral state, no highlights.

beats.text: Here's what we call the parts you're looking at. Watch.

params.description: Row 1 (top horizontal line of 4 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: See this line going across? That's a row. Rows go across, left to right.

params.description: Row 2 highlights, then row 3 highlights sequentially.

beats.text: Row 1, row 2, row 3. Three rows going across.

params.description: All row highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.
Column 1 (left vertical line of 3 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: Now this direction. See this line going up and down? That's a column. Columns go up and down.

params.description: Column 2, column 3, column 4 highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Column 1, column 2, column 3, column 4. Four columns, all up and down.

params.description: All column highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.

beats.text: Think about the difference. Rows go across. Columns go up and down. How many rows going across does this array have?

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.

beats.text: How many rows? Rows go across.

validator.description: Student selected 3, correct answer

beats.text: Right. 3 rows going across. So this array has 3 rows and 4 columns. An array is equal groups organized into rows and columns.

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.

validator.description: Student selected 4, counting columns instead of rows

params.description: All 3 rows highlight sequentially, left to right.

beats.text: You counted columns. Columns go up and down. Rows go across. Count the lines going across.

validator.description: Student selected 7, adding rows and columns together

params.description: All 3 rows highlight sequentially, left to right.

beats.text: The question asks for rows only, not the total. Rows are the lines going across. Count just the rows.

validator.description: Student selected 2, miscounting the rows

params.description: All 3 rows highlight sequentially, left to right.

beats.text: Let's count together. Rows go across, left to right. Watch each row light up as we count.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: All 3 rows highlight one at a time, with labels: row 1, row 2, row 3.

beats.text: Let me show you. I count the lines going across. One, two, three. Row 1, row 2, row 3. The answer is 3. Rows go across, and this array has 3 rows.

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. multiple_choice tool active.

params.description: 2×6 dot array appears. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Here's a new array, this time with dots. How many rows does this one have? Rows go across.
How many rows? (Rows go across.)

validator.description: Student answered 2, correct

beats.text: That's right. 2 rows. The lines going across.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

validator.description: Student selected 6: counted columns instead of rows

params.description: The 2 horizontal rows highlight.

beats.text: You counted the columns. Rows go across, like a line you'd read. How many lines go across?

validator.description: Student selected 8: likely added rows and columns

params.description: The 2 horizontal rows highlight.

beats.text: Let's think about this together. We're only counting rows right now. Rows go across. Count just the lines going across.

validator.description: Student selected 12: counted total dots

params.description: The 2 horizontal rows highlight.

beats.text: You counted the dots. We need to count the rows. A row is one line going across. How many lines go across?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts each row: first row across, then second row across.

beats.text: Let me show you how to count rows. I start at the top. First row across. Then the second row across. That's 2 rows total. Rows are the lines going across.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Now how many columns? Columns go up and down.
How many columns? (Columns go up and down.)

validator.description: Student answered 6, correct

beats.text: Yes. 6 columns. The lines going up and down. So this array has 2 rows and 6 columns.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

validator.description: Student selected 2: counted rows instead of columns

params.description: The 6 vertical columns highlight.

beats.text: You counted the rows. Columns go up and down, not across. How many lines go up and down?

validator.description: Student selected 8: likely added rows and columns

params.description: The 6 vertical columns highlight.

beats.text: Here's a hint: we're only counting columns now. Columns go up and down. Count just the lines going up and down.

validator.description: Student selected 12: counted total dots

params.description: The 6 vertical columns highlight.

beats.text: You counted the dots. We need to count the columns. A column is one line going up and down. How many lines go up and down?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts each column from left to right: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.

beats.text: This one takes a bit of thought, so let's work through it together. I start on the left. I count each line going up and down: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. That's 6 columns. Columns are the lines going up and down.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

params.description: 3×5 dot array appears. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.

beats.text: This array has 3 rows. Each row has 5 dots. We can say: 3 rows of 5.

Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially: row 1 (5 dots), row 2 (5 dots), row 3 (5 dots).

beats.text: Here's how that turns into a multiplication expression.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. Rows highlighted sequentially, animation complete.
Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. The first number tells us how many rows. That's 3.

params.description: 3 tile places into first slot of Equation Builder.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows [3] × [___]. First slot filled with 3.

beats.text: The second number tells us how many in each row. That's 5.

params.description: 5 tile places into second slot. Expression shows 3 × 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. That's just like saying 3 groups of 5. And you know equal groups means multiplication. So 3 rows of 5 becomes 3 times 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: Now here's a new array.

params.description: Equation Builder clears. Expression returns to [___] × [___].
3×5 array removed from screen.
New 4×2 dot array appears. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.

Equation Builder cleared, showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: Build the expression. First number is how many rows. Second is how many in each row.
Build the expression for this array.

validator.description: Student placed 4 × 2, correct

beats.text: Right. 4 times 2. That's 4 rows of 2. The words match the expression.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect expression on first attempt

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially: row 1, row 2, row 3, row 4. Each row has 2 dots.

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially with count: 1, 2, 3, 4 rows.
4 tile places into first slot.
2 tile places into second slot. Expression shows 4 × 2.

beats.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the rows: 1, 2, 3, 4. That's the first number. I count dots in one row: 2. That's the second number. The expression is 4 times 2. That's 4 rows of 2.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots. place_tile tool active.
Equation Builder showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears on screen. 2 rows, 5 columns of dots.

beats.text: You can describe the same array two ways -- by rows or by columns. Each way gives you a different expression. Now let's flip it. You're going to BUILD arrays. You tell the computer what to make, then find the answer.
